description_long: This sculpture is a rectangular stone relief featuring a prominent human figure with wings, possibly representing a mythological or angelic being. The figure stands upright within a recessed niche, with one arm raised and holding a draped object, while the other arm is extended downward. The wings are carved in high relief, arching behind the figure and adding depth to the composition. The surface of the stone is weathered, with visible chisel marks and some areas of erosion, particularly along the edges and corners. The back and sides of the sculpture are roughly hewn, contrasting with the more detailed front. The overall form suggests it may have once been part of a larger architectural or funerary monument.
description: A weathered stone relief sculpture of a winged human figure standing in a recessed niche, with detailed front and rough sides.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full winged figure in high relief, with clear details of the face, wings, and draped object.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing the depth of the niche and the rough, unfinished stonework on the side.
description_view_2: Rear-angled view showing the back of the niche and the figure's wings, with less detail visible on the figure itself.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the rough, uncarved stone and the protruding form of the figure's arm and wing.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
